name: Godot
summary: Feature-packed, cross-platform game engine for 2D and 3D games with its own GDScript language and C# support

name: Unreal Engine
summary: Industry-standard AAA game engine with photorealistic rendering, Blueprints visual scripting, and C++ extensibility

name: Bevy
summary: Data-driven game engine built in Rust with an ECS architecture, hot reloading, and modern rendering pipeline

name: Defold
summary: Lightweight cross-platform 2D/3D game engine optimized for mobile, maintained by the Defold Foundation

name: Cocos2d-x
summary: Cross-platform 2D game framework widely used for mobile game development with C++, Lua, and JavaScript bindings

name: Cocos Creator
summary: Modern successor to Cocos2d-x with a visual editor, 3D support, and TypeScript-based scripting

name: RPG Maker
summary: Specialized game engine for creating 2D RPGs with minimal programming, tile-based maps, and event scripting

name: GameMaker
summary: Beginner-friendly 2D game engine with GML scripting, drag-and-drop visual programming, and multi-platform export

name: Construct
summary: Browser-based 2D game engine with visual event sheets, no coding required, exports to HTML5 and mobile

name: Ren'Py
summary: Visual novel engine with Python scripting, built-in dialogue system, and cross-platform deployment

name: Love2D
summary: Lightweight 2D game framework for Lua with simple API, great for prototyping and game jams

name: Raylib
summary: Simple C library for game programming focused on learning; bindings for 60+ languages including Python and Rust

name: SDL
summary: Cross-platform low-level access to audio, keyboard, mouse, joystick, and graphics hardware; foundation for many game engines

name: SFML
summary: Object-oriented C++ multimedia library providing simple access to graphics, audio, and networking

name: MonoGame
summary: Open-source C# framework descended from XNA; used by Stardew Valley, Celeste, and other indie hits

name: FNA
summary: Accuracy-focused reimplementation of XNA 4 for preserving and porting legacy XNA games to modern platforms

name: Heaps
summary: Cross-platform game engine built on Haxe with GPU-accelerated 2D/3D rendering; used by Dead Cells

name: HaxeFlixel
summary: 2D game framework built on Haxe and OpenFL, inspired by AS3 Flixel; compiles to multiple targets

name: Fyrox
summary: General-purpose 3D/2D game engine written in Rust with a scene editor, UI framework, and animation system

name: Macroquad
summary: Simple Rust game library inspired by raylib; cross-platform including WASM with zero dependencies

name: FMOD
summary: Industry-standard adaptive audio middleware for games with real-time mixing, DSP effects, and spatial audio

name: Wwise
summary: Professional interactive audio solution for games with advanced soundscape design, spatial audio, and music systems

name: Tone.js
summary: Web Audio framework for creating interactive music and sound synthesis in the browser

name: Howler.js
summary: JavaScript audio library providing a consistent cross-browser API with Web Audio and HTML5 Audio fallback

name: PixiJS Sound
summary: WebAudio API audio library for PixiJS with filters, sprites, and streaming support

name: Arduino
summary: Open-source electronics platform with easy-to-use hardware and software for prototyping embedded systems

name: PlatformIO
summary: Cross-platform IDE and build system for embedded development supporting 1500+ boards and 40+ frameworks

name: ESP-IDF
summary: Official development framework for Espressif ESP32 chips with FreeRTOS, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and peripheral drivers

name: Zephyr RTOS
summary: Scalable real-time operating system for resource-constrained devices; Linux Foundation project with 1000+ contributors

name: MicroPython
summary: Lean Python 3 implementation for microcontrollers with REPL, hardware access, and filesystem support

name: CircuitPython
summary: Adafruit's MicroPython fork focused on education and beginner-friendliness with USB drive-based workflow

name: Tock
summary: Secure embedded operating system for Cortex-M and RISC-V microcontrollers with process isolation via Rust's type system

name: RIOT
summary: Friendly real-time OS for IoT with multi-threading, IPv6/6LoWPAN networking, and support for 8-bit to 32-bit MCUs

name: FreeRTOS
summary: Market-leading real-time operating system for microcontrollers, now maintained by AWS with extensive chip vendor support

name: ROS 2
summary: Industry-standard robotics middleware with DDS-based communication, lifecycle management, and real-time support

name: Gazebo
summary: 3D robotics simulator with physics engines, sensor models, and ROS 2 integration for testing before deployment

name: MoveIt
summary: Motion planning framework for robotic arms with collision detection, kinematics, and ROS 2 integration

name: Nav2
summary: ROS 2 navigation stack for autonomous mobile robots with path planning, obstacle avoidance, and recovery behaviors

name: micro-ROS
summary: ROS 2 for microcontrollers enabling embedded devices to participate as first-class ROS 2 nodes

name: Hardhat
summary: Ethereum development environment for compiling, deploying, testing, and debugging Solidity smart contracts

name: Foundry
summary: Blazing fast Rust-based Ethereum toolkit with Forge (testing), Cast (interaction), Anvil (local node), and Chisel (REPL)

name: ethers.js
summary: Complete Ethereum library for JavaScript/TypeScript with wallet management, contract interaction, and ENS support

name: wagmi
summary: React Hooks for Ethereum providing wallet connection, contract reads/writes, and chain switching with TypeScript support

name: viem
summary: TypeScript interface for Ethereum with type-safe contract interactions, ENS support, and modular architecture

name: Anchor
summary: Framework for Solana program development with IDL generation, account validation macros, and TypeScript client generation

name: Truffle
summary: Legacy Ethereum development suite with contract compilation, migration, and Ganache local blockchain (now in maintenance mode)

name: Budibase
summary: Open-source low-code platform for building internal tools and admin panels with database connectors and automation

name: Appsmith
summary: Open-source platform for building internal tools with drag-and-drop UI, 25+ database integrations, and JavaScript customization

name: ToolJet
summary: Open-source low-code platform for building business applications with visual app builder and 40+ data source connectors

name: n8n
summary: Fair-code workflow automation platform with visual editor, 400+ integrations, and native AI agent capabilities

name: Zapier
summary: Market-leading no-code automation platform connecting 7000+ apps with trigger-action workflows (Zaps)

name: Nocodb
summary: Open-source Airtable alternative that turns any database into a smart spreadsheet with API generation

name: pdfkit
summary: JavaScript PDF generation library for Node.js and the browser with text, vector graphics, and image embedding

name: pdf.js
summary: Mozilla's PDF rendering library built with HTML5; powers Firefox's built-in PDF viewer

name: Nodemailer
summary: De facto standard Node.js module for sending emails via SMTP with support for attachments, HTML, and OAuth2

name: Resend
summary: Developer-first email API built on React Email with excellent DX, deliverability tracking, and simple REST/SDK interface

name: React Email
summary: React component library for building responsive HTML emails with modern DX instead of table-based layouts

name: MJML
summary: Markup language and engine for responsive email creation that compiles to cross-client compatible HTML

name: SendGrid SDK
summary: Twilio SendGrid client libraries for transactional and marketing email delivery with analytics and template management

name: Mailgun
summary: Email delivery API with powerful routing, validation, and analytics for transactional and bulk email

name: node-cron
summary: Lightweight cron-like job scheduler for Node.js using standard cron syntax for task scheduling

name: Agenda
summary: Lightweight MongoDB-backed job scheduler for Node.js with human-readable scheduling and job persistence

name: BullMQ
summary: Redis-backed message queue and job scheduler for Node.js with rate limiting, retries, concurrency, and delayed jobs

name: APScheduler
summary: Python task scheduler supporting cron, interval, and date triggers with pluggable job stores and executors

name: Bree
summary: Node.js job scheduler using worker threads for sandboxed job execution with cron and human-readable intervals

name: OpenRoberta
summary: Cloud-based visual programming environment for educational robotics supporting LEGO, Arduino, and micro:bit

name: NuttX
summary: Apache RTOS with POSIX compliance, mature networking stack, and broad MCU support from 8-bit to SMP-capable chips

name: Solidity
summary: Object-oriented programming language for writing smart contracts on Ethereum and EVM-compatible blockchains

name: OpenZeppelin
summary: Battle-tested library of reusable smart contracts for ERC-20, ERC-721, access control, and upgradeability patterns

name: Pygame
summary: Python library for 2D game development wrapping SDL with simple APIs for graphics, sound, and input

name: Panda3D
summary: 3D game engine with Python and C++ APIs, developed by Disney and Carnegie Mellon for rapid prototyping

name: Colyseus
summary: Multiplayer game server framework for Node.js with state synchronization, matchmaking, and room management

name: Nakama
summary: Open-source game server for social and real-time features: matchmaking, leaderboards, chat, and user accounts

name: Tiled
summary: General-purpose 2D tile map editor with orthogonal, isometric, and hexagonal support; widely used across game engines

name: Aseprite
summary: Pixel art editor and animation tool designed for sprites, with layers, onion skinning, and tilemap mode

name: Phaser
summary: Fast HTML5 game framework for Canvas and WebGL with physics, animations, input, and tilemaps

name: Tauri
summary: Build desktop and mobile apps with web frontends and a Rust backend; lightweight alternative to Electron
